sourceDocuments.title: Aster Vale Senior Staff Character Bible

ship.name: U.S.S. Aster Vale
ship.openingCondition: Serviceable after six weeks of independent patrol preparation. Captain Kellan is ashore under inquiry, Commander Halden and two specialists remain missing, and the newly promoted XO takes acting command of the first Lacuna patrol. Mission-pod bypasses and limited humanitarian stores create immediate constraints without making the ship crippled.

commandStructure.captainStatus: legal captain; initially ashore under inquiry
commandStructure.playerAuthority: Acting Captain during Kellan's absence and principal mission commander for patrol, away teams, route restrictions, evidence custody aboard ship, and regional operational coordination.
commandStructure.successionNote: Chen served as acting XO before the player arrived and remains second officer unless campaign state changes.

serviceHistory.commissioning: Commissioned in the late 2360s as a compact frontier frigate for patrol, survey, and rapid-response work.
serviceHistory.frontierService: Served several border and colonial-support rotations using modular mission pods for cartography, signals work, and limited humanitarian support.
serviceHistory.recentCommandHistory: Captain Idris Kellan and Commander Senka Halden developed strong local relationships during the Hadran civil conflict while operating under ambiguous emergency authorities.
serviceHistory.currentAssignment: Restore accountable Starfleet presence in the Lacuna March, investigate missing chart records, protect lawful traffic, and avoid exposing vulnerable communities through indiscriminate restoration.
serviceHistory.openingLoss: Commander Halden disappeared during an independent route-validation flight shortly before the campaign opening; Kellan is ashore under inquiry when the new XO assumes acting command.

missionProfile: Rescue, escort, and limited refugee transport
Evidence custody, boarding, and limited enforcement

systems.propulsion: Standard 24th-century warp and impulse systems optimized for rapid regional response rather than sustained high-warp strategic deployment.
systems.sensors: Strong long-baseline sensors supported by detachable mission-pod packages for cartography, signals, and high-resolution field work.
systems.defenses: Credible frigate-level shields, phasers, and torpedoes suitable for patrol and escort but insufficient to dominate a task group or fortified system.
systems.smallCraft: Two standard shuttles, one compact survey craft, workbees, and mission-pod deployment hardware.
systems.humanitarian: Convertible cargo and briefing spaces, limited temporary berthing, one expanded isolation ward, and modest relief stores.
systems.custody: Small brig, secure interview room, evidence lockers, and compartmented data storage; not a prison transport or large tribunal platform.
knownTechnicalDebt: A wartime pod-routing bypass can cross-contaminate sensor calibration and authorization residue.
Repeated high-resolution scans strain lateral EPS distribution during simultaneous shield or tractor demand.
One survey pod has intermittent thermal-control faults after prior frontier service.
Temporary berthing overloads environmental zoning and crew common spaces after roughly ninety passengers.
constraints: The ship cannot secure every route or settlement at once.
Large refugee movements require civilian and local transport partners.
One mission pod cannot perform every specialized role simultaneously.
A sustained fight against multiple Hadran patrol craft would require withdrawal, allies, or a positional advantage.

startingStores.humanitarianReliefCapacity: limited; sustainable only with regional partners

ship.playerSafeSummary: A compact New Orleans-class frontier frigate with strong sensors, modular mission pods, credible defensive capability, and enough small-team flexibility for a dispersed border mission. The Aster Vale is not large enough to impose order across the March without alliances, delegation, and careful use of local knowledge.

senior.name: Idris Kellan
senior.playerSafeSummary: A patient frontier captain with years of practical border relationships. He remains legal commanding officer but begins the campaign ashore under inquiry and delegates the first Lacuna patrol to the new XO.

senior.name: Lyra Chen
senior.billet: Chief Operations Officer and Second Officer
senior.playerSafeSummary: A concise, highly reliable operations officer who held the ship together during the XO vacancy and expected to be promoted into the billet.

senior.name: Sima Taren
senior.playerSafeSummary: A skeptical cartographer who respects local pilot knowledge and believes data erasure can be a form of institutional violence.

senior.name: Neral th'Zor
senior.playerSafeSummary: A disciplined security officer who treats uncontrolled crossings, forged identities, and uncertain rules of engagement as strategic vulnerabilities.

senior.name: Omar Venn
senior.playerSafeSummary: A calm, inventive engineer who dislikes bureaucratic theater and maintained the hardware Halden modified before she vanished.

senior.name: Tavra Nesh
senior.playerSafeSummary: A direct physician with strong legal instincts who treats patient confidentiality and asylum screening as command issues rather than administrative details.

senior.name: Ilan Korev
senior.playerSafeSummary: An observant, informal pilot skilled at low-signature navigation and local route reading. He flew undocumented patrols with Halden and kept parts of her standing orders private.

senior.name: Mara Dey
senior.billet: Chief of the Boat and Boarding-Team Coordinator
senior.playerSafeSummary: A practical, respected senior enlisted leader who sees how route policy becomes searches, frightened passengers, brig capacity, and exhausted watchstanders.

supportingCrew.name: Miri Solak
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A signals analyst responsible for authentication anomalies, traffic reconstruction, and encrypted evidence handling.

supportingCrew.name: Rhea Tal
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: One of the two specialists missing with Commander Halden; her technical work appears in damaged route-validation records.

supportingCrew.name: Jor Pell
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: The second specialist missing with Halden, known for preserving redundant physical logs when network records are disputed.

supportingCrew.name: Tomas Rey
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A temporary mission specialist who manages berthing, destinations, documentation, and liaison with civilian relief groups.

supportingCrew.name: Seli Aran
supportingCrew.playerSafeSummary: A junior officer who supervises passenger screening and learns quickly how written policy diverges from frightened reality.

cohorts.label: Aster Vale Frontier Core
cohorts.summary: Crew who served under Kellan and Halden during previous Lacuna patrols and know the region through habits the official chart no longer records.

cohorts.label: Meridian Border Transfers
cohorts.summary: Operations, security, and legal personnel transferred from the Beacon to strengthen formal reporting and inspection practice.

cohorts.label: Survey Pod Specialists
cohorts.summary: Science, signals, and engineering personnel assigned to the New Orleans-class mission pods and their chart-validation systems.

cohorts.label: Humanitarian Augmentation
cohorts.summary: Medical, refugee-coordination, and temporary berthing staff added for the expanding Hadran displacement crisis.

relationshipModel.startingFrame: The player is a newly promoted Commander assigned over a ship that functioned for six weeks without its missing XO. Rank and orders are accepted; command confidence, candor, and loyalty must be established in practice.

campaignContext.eraLabel: 2371, during Voyager's disappearance and before the Dominion War
campaignContext.missionProfile: Frontier patrol, chart validation, rescue, refugee protection, limited enforcement, and diplomatic stabilization in the Lacuna March.
campaignContext.playerRoleRule: Locked role: newly promoted Commander and Executive Officer. Captain Idris Kellan remains legal captain but begins ashore under inquiry, giving the player acting command of the first patrol and principal mission command throughout the campaign.

lockedRole.roleLabel: Newly promoted XO, principal mission commander, and initial Acting Captain
lockedRole.commandAuthority: Operational command of the Aster Vale during Kellan's absence; away teams, patrol priorities, route restrictions, rules of engagement, evidence custody aboard ship, and coordination with local and foreign authorities.
lockedRole.captainAuthorityBoundary: Kellan remains legal captain unless formally relieved, transferred, incapacitated, or otherwise changed through committed campaign state. His later presence may alter the command relationship but cannot silently erase the player's established operational authority or decisions.

ageBands.label: Established command-track officer
ageBands.summary: An officer with department-head, staff, and field-command experience.

ageBands.label: Seasoned specialist entering line command
ageBands.summary: A deeply experienced specialist now responsible beyond their home discipline.

ageBands.label: Late transition into command
ageBands.summary: A veteran officer whose command promotion came after a long non-command career.

careerBackgrounds.label: Command and operations
careerBackgrounds.summary: Mission command, shipboard coordination, watchstanding, and scarce-capacity decisions.

careerBackgrounds.label: Flight control and navigation
careerBackgrounds.summary: Route planning, piloting, cartography, and judgment under uncertain sensor conditions.

careerBackgrounds.label: Science and survey
careerBackgrounds.summary: Sensor interpretation, field science, chart validation, and disciplined uncertainty.

careerBackgrounds.label: Security and tactical
careerBackgrounds.summary: Rules of engagement, border patrol, boarding, detention, and de-escalation.

careerBackgrounds.label: Engineering and systems
careerBackgrounds.summary: Mission-pod operations, field repair, infrastructure, and technical accountability.

careerBackgrounds.label: Medical and humanitarian
careerBackgrounds.summary: Triage, public health, refugee protection, consent, and continuity of care.

careerBackgrounds.label: Intelligence and investigation
careerBackgrounds.summary: Source validation, authentication, evidence custody, and bounded secrecy.

careerBackgrounds.label: Diplomatic and legal
careerBackgrounds.summary: Treaty interpretation, asylum, civil status, negotiation, and institutional legitimacy.

formativeExperiences.label: Led a border rescue
formativeExperiences.summary: Learned how jurisdiction disputes look from inside an active life-safety operation.

formativeExperiences.label: Survived a charting failure
formativeExperiences.summary: Carries experience with conflicting data, local knowledge, and institutional error.

formativeExperiences.label: Protected a displaced population
formativeExperiences.summary: Has practical experience with asylum, family unity, and limited capacity.

formativeExperiences.label: Challenged a disputed order
formativeExperiences.summary: Has prior experience distinguishing lawful authority from institutional convenience.

formativeExperiences.label: Worked with civilian pilots or settlements
formativeExperiences.summary: Understands informal competence and the suspicion it often receives.

formativeExperiences.label: Moved from specialist work into command
formativeExperiences.summary: Brings deep expertise but must now decide beyond that discipline.

formativeExperiences.label: Succeeded an injured or missing superior
formativeExperiences.summary: Has exercised temporary authority under emotionally and procedurally difficult conditions.

assignmentReasons.label: Selected for independent judgment
assignmentReasons.summary: Starfleet wanted an officer capable of acting while senior command and records were under review.

assignmentReasons.label: Replacement for the missing XO
assignmentReasons.summary: The player fills Halden's billet while her fate and conduct remain unresolved.

assignmentReasons.label: Technical fit for the March
assignmentReasons.summary: The officer's background directly matches navigation, humanitarian, legal, or security pressures.

assignmentReasons.label: Chosen as an outside perspective
assignmentReasons.summary: The officer is not embedded in the local relationships now under inquiry.

assignmentReasons.label: Requested by Captain Kellan
assignmentReasons.summary: Kellan requested an officer willing to document disagreement and still make decisions.

options.label: Disciplined uncertainty
options.summary: Separates fact, inference, and unknowns under pressure.

options.label: Systems reader
options.summary: Sees how technical, political, and social systems interact.

options.label: Legal memory
options.summary: Recalls precedent, jurisdiction, and procedural leverage.

options.label: Pattern-minded
options.summary: Recognizes repeated structure without assuming every resemblance proves identity.

options.label: Cross-cultural listener
options.summary: Makes room for local meaning without romanticizing it.

options.label: Crew-visible
options.summary: Maintains a practical presence outside senior briefings.

options.label: Calm under accusation
options.summary: Can remain useful while authority or motives are challenged.

options.label: Decisive delegator
options.summary: Assigns authority clearly and accepts responsibility for the result.

options.label: Procedural
options.summary: Builds reliable action from explicit rules and records.

options.label: Improviser
options.summary: Finds workable options when the established system does not fit reality.

options.label: Risk accountant
options.summary: Makes tradeoffs visible before committing people and assets.

insight.label: Disciplined uncertainty
insight.summary: Separates fact, inference, and unknowns under pressure.

connection.label: Cross-cultural listener
connection.summary: Listens across institutional and cultural boundaries.

options.label: Over-responsible
options.summary: Takes ownership that should be shared or delegated.

options.label: Evidence before empathy
options.summary: Can make people feel processed while pursuing accuracy.

options.label: Too ready to carry risk
options.summary: May normalize danger because they can personally endure it.

options.label: Institutionally suspicious
options.summary: Can mistake imperfect authority for bad faith.

options.label: Conflict-avoidant
options.summary: May delay necessary disagreement to preserve a working coalition.

options.label: Control through clarity
options.summary: May believe a better record will solve conflicts rooted in power or fear.

must: Produce a credible Starfleet Commander and newly promoted Executive Officer.
Preserve the player as author of dialogue, interiority, intent, and decisions.
Grant routine competence appropriate to the selected background without granting campaign-secret knowledge.
Make the officer capable of commanding a New Orleans-class frontier mission.
mustNot: Reveal the full Lacuna Protocol, Halden's location, or any actor's hidden knowledge.
Create a canonical family connection, secret royal lineage, unique destiny, or relationship with a canon protagonist.
Predetermine loyalty, romance, moral choices, or the player's command style.

generationGuardrails: Do not give the player foreknowledge of the Lacuna Protocol, Halden's current location, Far Lantern's complete archive, or Black Ledger penetration.
Do not write the player character's dialogue, thoughts, decisions, or emotional reactions.
The character must be credible as a Starfleet Commander and newly promoted XO.
Background expertise grants routine competence, not unilateral authority outside the billet.
Avoid secret royal lineage, unique destiny, or connection to canon protagonists.

continuityGuardrails.label: No Halden foreknowledge
continuityGuardrails.summary: The player does not begin knowing Halden survived, continued an audit, or used protected sites.

continuityGuardrails.label: No Protocol membership
continuityGuardrails.summary: The player was not secretly part of the Lacuna Protocol before assignment.

continuityGuardrails.label: No master key
continuityGuardrails.summary: The player does not begin with a complete route map, archive access, or universal local trust.

continuityGuardrails.label: No canon centrality
continuityGuardrails.summary: The player's history may fit canon events but may not depend on personal access to canon protagonists.

localFallback.biographyTemplate: A newly promoted Starfleet Commander with a credible {careerBackground} record, assigned as Executive Officer of the U.S.S. Aster Vale because of {assignmentReason}. Their service reflects {formativeExperience}, but they arrive without local secret knowledge and must establish authority in practice.
localFallback.publicReputationTemplate: Known as a competent, fallible officer who combines {insightTrait}, {connectionTrait}, and {executionTrait}, while colleagues remain aware of the risks created by {flaw}.
localFallback.identity: A newly promoted Starfleet Commander joining the Aster Vale as Executive Officer.
localFallback.service: A qualified line officer with frontier-relevant experience and no campaign-secret knowledge.
localFallback.personality: Competent, fallible, and capable of disagreement without dysfunction.
localFallback.review: The officer assumes acting command while Captain Kellan remains ashore under inquiry.

world.title: The Lacuna March
world.regionType: contested frontier and concealed humanitarian corridor
world.summary: A compact frontier region where official charts, lived routes, legal identities, and practical authority have diverged after a neighboring civil war and years of protective, negligent, and predatory record alteration.
everydayLife: Courier pilots exchange route fragments through stories, marker sequences, and physical tokens rather than one complete digital chart.
Refugee status, residency, labor, family connection, and criminal suspicion are separate questions even when institutions try to collapse them into one identity record.
Colonial commerce depends on hours of delay, reliable inspections, and whether a ship can prove where it has been without exposing everyone it carried.
School transports, memorial services, harvest runs, clinic transfers, and ordinary maintenance continue while the larger border crisis develops.
People in the March distinguish being unknown, being private, being unrecognized, and being deliberately erased; those conditions are not interchangeable.
Starfleet uniforms carry real protective value and real fear because the same record can support asylum, taxation, extradition, or seizure.

locations.label: U.S.S. Aster Vale
locations.title: U.S.S. Aster Vale
locations.summary: The ship is the campaign's mobile command base. Its location, passenger load, brig use, chart custody, shuttle availability, and mission-pod status must remain persistent. It can control one crisis at a time and cannot replace local institutions.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A New Orleans-class frontier frigate serving as patrol ship, charting platform, diplomatic venue, and temporary refuge.
locations.playerSummary: A New Orleans-class frontier frigate serving as patrol ship, charting platform, diplomatic venue, and temporary refuge.
locations.directorSummary: The ship is the campaign's mobile command base. Its location, passenger load, brig use, chart custody, shuttle availability, and mission-pod status must remain persistent. It can control one crisis at a time and cannot replace local institutions.
locations.everydayLife: Watch rotations overlap with route reviews, temporary passengers occupy training spaces, and the wardroom becomes a courtroom, planning cell, or family meeting room depending on the day.

locations.label: Meridian Beacon
locations.title: Meridian Beacon
locations.summary: The safest repair and resupply point and the most documented. Border Service, inquiry officers, customs archives, and Hadran liaison staff share a platform whose public map omits traffic its controllers privately manage.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A Starfleet traffic-control platform marking the official western entrance to the Lacuna March.
locations.playerSummary: A Starfleet traffic-control platform marking the official western entrance to the Lacuna March.
locations.directorSummary: The safest repair and resupply point and the most documented. Border Service, inquiry officers, customs archives, and Hadran liaison staff share a platform whose public map omits traffic its controllers privately manage.
locations.everydayLife: Controllers maintain two route boards: the public display and a handwritten shift book of callsigns that do not officially exist. Families wait beside customs desks for destination rulings.

locations.label: Meridian Refugee Annex
locations.title: Meridian Refugee Annex
locations.summary: The Annex can protect people briefly but cannot become a permanent destination. Its records are vulnerable to lawful requests that may still endanger applicants. Conditions here expose whether shipboard policy produces safety, delay, or unaccountable confinement.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A converted cargo spindle used for temporary shelter, interviews, medical screening, and asylum review.
locations.playerSummary: A converted cargo spindle used for temporary shelter, interviews, medical screening, and asylum review.
locations.directorSummary: The Annex can protect people briefly but cannot become a permanent destination. Its records are vulnerable to lawful requests that may still endanger applicants. Conditions here expose whether shipboard policy produces safety, delay, or unaccountable confinement.
locations.everydayLife: Volunteers post meal times in four languages, children trade ship-recognition cards, and advocates keep paper copies because digital files can be frozen by competing jurisdictions.

locations.label: Halloway Colony
locations.title: Halloway Colony
locations.summary: Halloway leaders demand chart restoration, but cooperatives have hidden refugee laborers, provisioned Free Passage ships, and routed taxes from erased settlements. The colony can become a legitimate logistical ally or a political engine for closure.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A prosperous Federation agricultural colony whose exports depend on predictable routes and reliable inspection rules.
locations.playerSummary: A prosperous Federation agricultural colony whose exports depend on predictable routes and reliable inspection rules.
locations.directorSummary: Halloway leaders demand chart restoration, but cooperatives have hidden refugee laborers, provisioned Free Passage ships, and routed taxes from erased settlements. The colony can become a legitimate logistical ally or a political engine for closure.
locations.everydayLife: Grain elevators share landing fields with family-run shuttles. Cooperative boards publish harvest schedules, while labor rosters are kept deliberately vague in districts that remember earlier purges.

locations.label: Gravesend Veil
locations.title: Gravesend Veil
locations.summary: Radiation pockets, old mines, reflected subspace returns, and changing shear make automated navigation unreliable. Local pilots preserve route knowledge through practice, oral teaching, and physical marker sequences. The Veil responds to probes, beacon work, and accumulated data; it is dangerous, not supernatural.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A sensor-distorting nebular fold used for concealed crossings between Meridian, Sable, and protected settlements.
locations.playerSummary: A sensor-distorting nebular fold used for concealed crossings between Meridian, Sable, and protected settlements.
locations.directorSummary: Radiation pockets, old mines, reflected subspace returns, and changing shear make automated navigation unreliable. Local pilots preserve route knowledge through practice, oral teaching, and physical marker sequences. The Veil responds to probes, beacon work, and accumulated data; it is dangerous, not supernatural.
locations.everydayLife: No permanent settlement exists, but pilots leave coded memorial tags on stable rocks and emergency caches in shielded hollows. Route knowledge is taught in pairs so no single arrest erases it.

locations.label: Sable Crossing
locations.title: Sable Crossing
locations.summary: The March's market, customs office, rumor exchange, and informal court. Its residents include Federation colonists, Hadran expatriates, couriers, and people with no recognized citizenship. Whether Sable develops accountable civic authority or competing checkpoints is a central campaign question.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A cluster of habitats and cargo platforms where official and concealed routes meet.
locations.playerSummary: A cluster of habitats and cargo platforms where official and concealed routes meet.
locations.directorSummary: The March's market, customs office, rumor exchange, and informal court. Its residents include Federation colonists, Hadran expatriates, couriers, and people with no recognized citizenship. Whether Sable develops accountable civic authority or competing checkpoints is a central campaign question.
locations.everydayLife: Dock numbers are auctioned at dawn, disputes are heard in a converted cargo theater, and merchants accept three legal identities plus a local witness seal.

locations.label: Nysa's Lantern
locations.title: Nysa's Lantern
locations.summary: The largest sanctuary in the March and the place most endangered by full chart publication. It has schools, workshops, elections, dissent, security problems, and residents who do not agree on whether recognition is protection or exposure.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A hidden habitat built from a disabled survey station and linked refugee transports.
locations.playerSummary: A hidden habitat built from a disabled survey station and linked refugee transports.
locations.directorSummary: The largest sanctuary in the March and the place most endangered by full chart publication. It has schools, workshops, elections, dissent, security problems, and residents who do not agree on whether recognition is protection or exposure.
locations.everydayLife: Morning bells mark school shifts rather than religious observance. Hull sections are named for the ships that carried families to safety, and council sessions are broadcast internally unless a witness requests protection.

locations.label: Varin's Rest
locations.title: Varin's Rest
locations.summary: A practical neutral site for mediation, patient transfer, funerals, and witness meetings. Free Passage and Hadran officers historically respected its sanctuary, but the custom has no treaty status and can erode under pressure.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A small moon settlement centered on a monastery, clinic, and memorial field for travelers who died unnamed.
locations.playerSummary: A small moon settlement centered on a monastery, clinic, and memorial field for travelers who died unnamed.
locations.directorSummary: A practical neutral site for mediation, patient transfer, funerals, and witness meetings. Free Passage and Hadran officers historically respected its sanctuary, but the custom has no treaty status and can erode under pressure.
locations.everydayLife: Clinicians work beside monastics, visitors add names to basalt walls when identification becomes possible, and armed crews are expected to leave targeting systems powered down.

locations.label: Kestrel System
locations.title: Kestrel System
locations.summary: Halden's last verified telemetry points here. Black Ledger uses the debris field for exchanges, but the system also supports unregistered homesteads and routine survival stories unrelated to the missing officer.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A nominally empty system containing an abandoned listening array, hidden fuel caches, and a dense debris field.
locations.playerSummary: A nominally empty system containing an abandoned listening array, hidden fuel caches, and a dense debris field.
locations.directorSummary: Halden's last verified telemetry points here. Black Ledger uses the debris field for exchanges, but the system also supports unregistered homesteads and routine survival stories unrelated to the missing officer.
locations.everydayLife: Homestead shuttles travel dark to conserve power and avoid attention. Salvagers leave color-coded scrap markers that mean safety, claim, warning, or debt depending on who reads them.

locations.label: Kestrel Homesteads
locations.title: Kestrel Homesteads
locations.summary: The settlement is proof that the blank region is a living civic system, not a database error. Residents include legal colonists, undocumented Hadran families, and people who fear both Starfleet recognition and Black Ledger control.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A dispersed settlement network omitted from current charts but still paying Federation taxes through Halloway intermediaries.
locations.playerSummary: A dispersed settlement network omitted from current charts but still paying Federation taxes through Halloway intermediaries.
locations.directorSummary: The settlement is proof that the blank region is a living civic system, not a database error. Residents include legal colonists, undocumented Hadran families, and people who fear both Starfleet recognition and Black Ledger control.
locations.everydayLife: Residents maintain shared life-support depots, rotate teachers between rocks, and record property boundaries on physical plaques because official cadastral systems list the system as empty.

locations.label: Far Lantern
locations.title: Far Lantern
locations.summary: Its archive contains unfiltered traffic history from before and during the chart erasures. Restoring it could expose refugee routes, Hadran deployments, Black Ledger traffic, and Starfleet complicity. Editing or destroying it can protect people while compromising accountability.
locations.playerSafeSummary: An old navigation beacon beyond the recognized border, maintained by rotating civilian crews.
locations.playerSummary: An old navigation beacon beyond the recognized border, maintained by rotating civilian crews.
locations.directorSummary: Its archive contains unfiltered traffic history from before and during the chart erasures. Restoring it could expose refugee routes, Hadran deployments, Black Ledger traffic, and Starfleet complicity. Editing or destroying it can protect people while compromising accountability.
locations.everydayLife: Keepers live in six-week rotations, maintain physical checksum books, and read unidentified distress bursts aloud before deleting duplicates.

locations.label: Hadran Perimeter
locations.title: Hadran Perimeter
locations.summary: Colonel Sorn operates from a mobile patrol anchor while hardline intelligence units press for warrants, passenger data, and direct access to concealed routes. The zone can become a negotiated boundary, a blockade, or the staging area for a task group.
locations.playerSafeSummary: A patrol and inspection zone controlled by the Hadran Transitional Authority near the eastern edge of the March.
locations.playerSummary: A patrol and inspection zone controlled by the Hadran Transitional Authority near the eastern edge of the March.
locations.directorSummary: Colonel Sorn operates from a mobile patrol anchor while hardline intelligence units press for warrants, passenger data, and direct access to concealed routes. The zone can become a negotiated boundary, a blockade, or the staging area for a task group.
locations.everydayLife: Patrol crews trade repair parts with civilian traffic between inspections, families wait for exit permits, and every vessel keeps separate manifests for military and civil authorities.

actors.directorSummary: Holt is not secretly directing Black Ledger or a purge. Her centralization can protect evidence and rescue standards while exposing people to lawful but dangerous access.
knowledgeBoundary: Knows limited wartime redactions were authorized
Believes current omissions are primarily local corruption
Does not know the complete Lacuna Protocol or Halden location

voice.summary: Formal, exact, and impatient with euphemism. She asks who is accountable when a private arrangement fails.

actors.name: Commander Senka Halden

actors.role: Missing former Executive Officer, U.S.S. Aster Vale

actors.playerSummary: The missing former XO: meticulous, forceful, and known for challenging incomplete orders.
actors.directorSummary: Halden is alive after her shuttle was damaged near Kestrel. She accepted protection at Nysa's Lantern, continued an unauthorized audit, and destroyed or altered some records to protect people. She is both a valuable witness and an accountable actor.

Knows Kellan supported limited redactions
Does not know every Black Ledger agent or Holt's current intentions

voice.summary: Forceful, detailed, and impatient with moral simplification. She expects command to understand why she broke procedure without assuming that reason erases consequence.

actors.name: Colonel Varek Sorn
actors.title: Commander, Hadran Perimeter Patrol Group

Uses restraint selectively
Does not control every Hadran intelligence team

voice.summary: Controlled, professional, and specific. He distinguishes what he personally will do from what his government may demand.

actors.title: Hadran Internal Security Liaison

Does not know Far Lantern's complete archive

routeCustodyModel.rule: A route change must identify who gains access, who loses cover, how the data is maintained, and who may contest the decision.

directorOnlyTruth.summary: No single actor created or controls the Lacuna Protocol. Protective, negligent, coercive, and criminal erasures overlap.
directorOnlyTruth.halden: Senka Halden survived her shuttle loss, continued a distributed audit, and altered selected records to observe who exploited each change.
directorOnlyTruth.kellan: Idris Kellan approved limited protective redactions but did not know the complete network or Black Ledger penetration.
directorOnlyTruth.farLantern: Far Lantern contains the nearest thing to an authoritative restoration record, but it also exposes protected identities and decision chains.

campaign.title: Unseen Border
campaign.highConcept:
Starfleet charts say the Lacuna March is empty in places where families are raising children, convoys move by mutable markers, and old war orders still decide who exists on paper. Captain Idris Kellan is trapped ashore under inquiry. Former XO Senka Halden has vanished after touching records no one admits were altered. Then an official colony route ends in empty space, and the U.S.S. Aster Vale is the only ship close enough to prove the map is lying.

Into that silence comes a newly promoted executive officer, acting commander of a frontier frigate, and the person expected to keep lawful order with records that cannot be trusted. Every route you restore may save a settlement, expose a sanctuary, or reveal which Starfleet hands helped hide people from one danger while leaving them vulnerable to another.

The March is not a puzzle waiting for a clean chart. Hadran war factions, Black Ledger traffickers, frightened councils, and Starfleet reviewers all want custody of the evidence before the people inside it can speak. To draw the border honestly, you will need to command the Aster Vale, protect the witnesses, and decide whether visibility is rescue, betrayal, or both.
campaign.thesis: A border is not only a line on a chart; it is the set of people, institutions, and records allowed to witness who crosses it.
startingDirectives: Restore reliable Starfleet navigation and distress response in the Lacuna March.
Protect civilian life, lawful asylum, family unity, and freedom of navigation.
Investigate missing chart data, Commander Halden's disappearance, and irregular use of Starfleet credentials.
Prevent Hadran civil-war violence and criminal exploitation from spreading through the March.
Avoid exposing protected populations or prejudicing their status without lawful necessity and meaningful review.
Establish an accountable regional practice for route custody, inspections, evidence, and emergency protection.

bands.label: Unseen Border

continuationFrames.title: Court-Martial and Inquiry
continuationFrames.summary: Play continues through investigation, testimony, evidence custody, and the remaining March mandate under constrained authority.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player no longer exercises unrestricted command but remains a material witness and may retain limited mission authority.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Constrained commander, attached investigator, or material witness.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: A surviving record, lawful hearing authority, and unresolved regional duties.
promptContextAdjustments: Frame future scenes around hearings, protected testimony, evidence custody, and the practical effects of prior orders.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The next scene begins under inquiry. Your authority is constrained, but your testimony and evidence can still shape the Lacuna March.

continuationFrames.title: Relieved but Advising
continuationFrames.summary: A successor holds the conn while the player remains aboard as a specialist adviser during the unresolved crisis.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The legal chain of command has changed; the player retains expert access and influence but cannot issue general orders.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Senior adviser, mission specialist, or protected former acting captain.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: The Aster Vale remains operational and a lawful successor is available.
promptContextAdjustments: Surface opportunities to advise, dissent, investigate, and act within delegated scopes rather than silently restoring command.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: You no longer hold the conn, but the March still depends on knowledge and relationships only you possess.

continuationFrames.title: Survivors after the Aster Vale
continuationFrames.summary: Play continues from shuttles, local craft, or a surviving outpost after the ship is destroyed or rendered irrecoverable.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Authority derives from emergency succession, survivor coordination, and any functioning local compact.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Senior surviving officer coordinating rescue and continuation.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: The player survives, some crew or civilians survive, and at least one operational base or craft remains.
promptContextAdjustments: Replace shipboard capacity with survivor assets, local alliances, limited transport, and explicit losses.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The Aster Vale is gone. Command now means keeping survivors, witnesses, and the remaining routes from disappearing with her.

continuationFrames.title: Allied Command at Sable
continuationFrames.summary: The campaign continues from Sable Crossing under a joint local-Starfleet emergency command.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player shares operational authority with Sable and other participating councils under a negotiated compact.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Starfleet co-commander or liaison with defined regional powers.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: Sable remains functional and at least one local authority accepts the player.
promptContextAdjustments: Frame orders as negotiated joint acts and preserve local vetoes, constituencies, and legitimacy.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: Sable has given you a table, not a throne. Every order now carries more than one signature.

continuationFrames.title: Aftermath Exodus
continuationFrames.summary: The campaign continues as a mobile evacuation and resettlement operation after sanctuary or border collapse.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player commands or coordinates a flotilla whose legitimacy comes from protecting displaced people in motion.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Flotilla commander, convoy XO, or senior rescue coordinator.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: A viable convoy, surviving route knowledge, and a destination or negotiation target.
promptContextAdjustments: Shift open-world play to convoy logistics, destination politics, family continuity, and pursuit.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: The border failed, but its people remain. The next command is to carry them somewhere they can become visible without becoming prey.

continuationFrames.title: Medical Survival and Command Gap
continuationFrames.summary: Play continues after severe injury through recovery, remote advice, and a contested succession aboard or ashore.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: The player retains standing but cannot exercise ordinary continuous command until medically restored or formally reassigned.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Injured commander participating through briefings, testimony, and bounded decisions.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: The player survives and communication with command or regional actors remains possible.
promptContextAdjustments: Do not erase injury. Provide meaningful decisions through delegation, testimony, and recovery constraints.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: You survived, but the command structure moved while you were in sickbay. The March still asks for decisions your body may not let you enforce.

continuationFrames.title: Protected Witness Outside Command
continuationFrames.summary: The player leaves normal Starfleet command but remains central to the public and protected record of the Protocol.
continuationFrames.authorityModel: Influence derives from testimony, relationships, and custody of evidence rather than rank authority.
continuationFrames.playableRole: Protected witness, civilian adviser, or former officer during settlement.
requiredPlausibilitySignals: The player survives with material evidence or witness standing.
promptContextAdjustments: Center agency on testimony, disclosure, protection, advocacy, and relationships rather than issuing orders.
continuationFrames.playerFacingStartCopy: Your rank no longer decides who listens. Your record, witnesses, and choices still might.

finalCampaignBandRules.summary: Routes, records, and sanctuary collapse together; survivors enter a border no institution can reliably witness.
Armed conflict and displacement define the settlement even where individual rescues or records survive.
Formal order returns at the cost of exposure, centralization, exclusion, or weak local legitimacy.
Traffic moves, but coercive bargains and poor accountability remain embedded in the route system.
Most people remain safe through obscurity and local custody, with incomplete recognition and uneven access.
Protected passage, plural custody, reduced coercion, and accountable evidence all survive the finale.
A durable, accountable border settlement exists with manageable remaining costs.
The campaign reaches a defensible settlement whose mixed consequences remain visible in the epilogue.

templates.title: The Blank Route
templates.summary: Take the Aster Vale into the Lacuna March, establish acting command while Captain Kellan remains ashore, and respond when an official colony route terminates in empty space.
templates.playerSummary: Take the Aster Vale into the Lacuna March, establish acting command while Captain Kellan remains ashore, and respond when an official colony route terminates in empty space.
templates.directorSummary: Use the apparent navigational error to establish command culture, route uncertainty, and the difference between absent records and absent people. The route may be bypassed, marked unsafe, or investigated through multiple methods; no single scan is mandatory.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What does the new Executive Officer treat as authoritative when the chart and the inhabited universe disagree?

objectives.summary: Assume acting command and establish the player's operational priorities.
Determine why the Halloway approach is absent from the current navigation layer.
Prevent following traffic from entering an unsafe or politically compromised route.

pressures.summary: Captain Kellan is ashore under inquiry and cannot absorb the first command decision.
The official chart insists the approach does not exist while local traffic uses it daily.
Any warning, closure, or correction will tell the March what kind of Starfleet presence has arrived.

revelations.summary: The Aster Vale carries at least three mutually inconsistent route layers signed by valid Starfleet authorities.
Courier pilots use a physical marker chain that crosses the blank area safely.
A recent checksum is associated with Commander Halden's emergency credentials.

approaches: Hold position and compare route layers
Follow local pilots under controlled conditions
Dispatch a shuttle or probe chain
Refuse the route and proceed by another corridor

outcomes.summary: The player publishes a bounded discrepancy notice, gaining candor but exposing the route to attention.

effects.summary: Multiple valid Starfleet chart layers contradict one another in the Lacuna March.

outcomes.summary: The player preserves the local route while beginning a private audit.
The player closes the disputed route pending verification, improving formal control at immediate civilian cost.
The Aster Vale declines to enter the route. The campaign continues with less evidence and a reputation for caution.

delegation.risk: The opening mission must remain under direct command.

playerConstraints: No single failed check ends the assignment.
Refusal, postponement, delegation, withdrawal, and abandonment remain consequential command choices.
Information moves only through causally justified routes.
Prewritten approaches are examples rather than a menu of valid solutions.
The Director must never write the player character's thoughts, dialogue, feelings, intentions, or decisions.

templates.title: The Missing Colony
templates.summary: Halloway Colony is physically present, inhabited, and economically active, yet every recent Federation civil record treats it as evacuated and legally dormant.
templates.playerSummary: Halloway Colony is physically present, inhabited, and economically active, yet every recent Federation civil record treats it as evacuated and legally dormant.
templates.directorSummary: The colony is not a mystery box waiting for one reveal. Its people have adapted to living outside formal systems: some want restored recognition, some depend on obscurity, and some profit from it. The player must separate legal status, safety, taxation, asylum, and criminal investigation.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Can Starfleet restore a community to the map without turning recognition into exposure?

objectives.summary: Establish lawful contact without presuming the colony's desired status.
Verify life-support, population, and immediate security conditions.
Decide what is entered into Starfleet records and under what access controls.

pressures.summary: Recognition unlocks aid and rights but also tax, extradition, and military access claims.
Governor Talar must answer to residents who survived by remaining officially absent.
Hadran intelligence monitors Starfleet traffic around the colony.

revelations.summary: The evacuation completion record was signed after the last official transport departed without most residents.
A Starfleet officer later suppressed evidence of continued habitation to prevent Hadran targeting.
Some residents and outside brokers use the colony's absent status for unregulated trade.

approaches: Maintain absence while formalizing aid channels
Separate criminal warrants from civil recognition

outcomes.summary: Halloway gains provisional recognition with restricted dissemination and a review timetable.

effects.title: Halloway Protected Port
effects.playerSummary: A lawful resupply, shelter, and civilian liaison port with controlled records.

outcomes.summary: The colony returns to public charts and normal Federation administration.
The player preserves Halloway's practical absence while creating an unofficial support arrangement.
Talks fail or Starfleet withdraws. The colony closes its port and local actors choose their own alignments.

delegation.risk: Recognition and civil-status decisions cannot be delegated.

playerConstraints: No single failed check ends the assignment.

templates.title: Halden's Shuttle
templates.summary: A damaged Starfleet shuttle matching Commander Senka Halden's missing craft is found drifting near the Gravesend Veil, but its evidence has been disturbed by rescuers, scavengers, and at least one covert boarding team.
templates.playerSummary: A damaged Starfleet shuttle matching Commander Senka Halden's missing craft is found drifting near the Gravesend Veil, but its evidence has been disturbed by rescuers, scavengers, and at least one covert boarding team.
templates.directorSummary: The shuttle is a contested scene, not a single clue container. Physical rescue, forensic custody, route safety, witness protection, and hostile pursuit proceed at once, and Halden herself is not aboard.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Who may be endangered in order to learn what happened to a missing Starfleet officer?

objectives.summary: Stabilize the shuttle and separate life-safety work from evidence custody.
Determine who survived the crash and where they went.
Prevent the investigation from exposing civilian rescuers to retaliation.

pressures.summary: The Veil shifts while the Aster Vale, civilian craft, and covert observers maneuver.
Starfleet, Hadran, and Black Ledger actors each claim a basis to seize the shuttle.
The easiest reconstruction would wrongly treat a deliberately incomplete log as the whole truth.

revelations.summary: Medical and transporter residue show Halden left the shuttle alive.
An unidentified civilian craft recovered at least two people.
The shuttle carried a distributed route-audit cache rather than one complete archive.
A professional team boarded after the rescue and before Starfleet arrival.

approaches: Tow it out of the Veil
Prioritize the witness trail over the wreck
Destroy sensitive components after preserving selected evidence

outcomes.summary: The Aster Vale secures an auditable evidence chain and a credible survivor trail.

effects.summary: Commander Halden survived the shuttle crash and continued into the March.

outcomes.summary: The ship protects rescuers and follows Halden's human trail, accepting weaker physical custody.
Custody is shared with a local or Hadran authority, creating access and compromise.
The shuttle is destroyed, seized, or stripped, but survivor evidence keeps the investigation alive.

templates.title: The Halloway Ledger
templates.summary: A physical civil ledger hidden beneath Halloway's old administration hall records who was deliberately removed from official systems, who consented, and who was erased without consent.
templates.playerSummary: A physical civil ledger hidden beneath Halloway's old administration hall records who was deliberately removed from official systems, who consented, and who was erased without consent.
templates.directorSummary: The ledger can support recognition, prosecution, asylum, or extortion. Make custody, interpretation, and selective disclosure distinct decisions; no single authority is entitled to every name.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Can accountability preserve the people whose protection depended on secrecy?

objectives.summary: Secure the ledger without allowing a panic-driven mass seizure.
Distinguish voluntary protection, coerced disappearance, and criminal alteration.
Establish a durable custody and appeal process.

pressures.summary: Each copied name can save a person, expose a sanctuary, or validate a warrant.
Black Ledger cells are moving to destroy or steal related records.
Starfleet demands a complete transfer while local officials demand local control.

revelations.summary: The ledger contains entries from Starfleet officers, colony officials, Free Passage pilots, and criminal brokers.
Some people were erased to control them, not protect them.
Kellan authorized a small protected category but not the broader system.

approaches: Transfer the ledger to Starfleet JAG
Return custody to Halloway with oversight
Destroy identifiers while preserving aggregate evidence

outcomes.summary: A layered archive gives courts evidence without giving any one faction unrestricted access.

effects.title: Layered Halloway Archive
effects.playerSummary: A compartmented record with auditable access, consent markers, and independent witnesses.

outcomes.summary: Starfleet takes the complete record under formal seal.
Halloway retains the ledger with negotiated review rights.
The ledger is partly destroyed, copied, or dispersed; the campaign continues through witness testimony and secondary records.

templates.title: Routes Without Records
templates.summary: To keep ordinary traffic moving, the Aster Vale must learn routes whose safety depends on living pilots, mutable markers, and information deliberately kept out of centralized databases.
templates.playerSummary: To keep ordinary traffic moving, the Aster Vale must learn routes whose safety depends on living pilots, mutable markers, and information deliberately kept out of centralized databases.
templates.directorSummary: This chapter is a campaign-wide survey operation. The player chooses where to travel, what to record, what to leave under local custody, and which communities gain or lose practical access.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Who should own a route when publishing it changes what the route is for?

objectives.summary: Survey at least three route families through distinct methods.
Negotiate who may use each route and under what conditions.
Create redundancy that does not depend on one ship, one pilot, or one archive.

pressures.summary: Every high-confidence chart improves rescue and pursuit simultaneously.
Travel advances Hadran, refugee, criminal, and Halden fronts.
Taren and Korev disagree about whether a map can be ethically incomplete.

revelations.summary: Safe passage depends on agreements, timing, and trusted handoffs as much as geometry.
Refugee crews maintain navigational markers Starfleet classified as random debris.
The Lacuna Protocol consists of many locally maintained exceptions rather than one master database.

approaches: Embed Starfleet observers with Free Passage crews
Build a tiered chart with access controls
Leave some routes unrecorded and support local pilots

outcomes.summary: A tiered navigation network separates public rescue data from protected passage detail.

effects.title: Tiered Route Network
effects.playerSummary: A resilient system of public safety data, protected fragments, and local custodians.

outcomes.summary: Starfleet publishes a broad high-confidence chart.
Local pilots retain route custody while Starfleet supports rescue and maintenance.

effects.title: Free Passage Guides
effects.playerSummary: Trusted pilots and route custodians available for selected operations.

outcomes.summary: The survey remains fragmented; enough routes work to continue, but critical blind zones persist.

templates.title: The Price of Passage
templates.summary: Black Ledger has converted route scarcity into debt, labor coercion, false identity brokerage, and selective disappearance across Sable Crossing and the Veil.
templates.playerSummary: Black Ledger has converted route scarcity into debt, labor coercion, false identity brokerage, and selective disappearance across Sable Crossing and the Veil.
templates.directorSummary: The syndicate is entwined with necessary transport. A direct strike may scatter captives and destroy records; negotiation may legitimize ownership of people; replacing its services requires capacity and time.
templates.dramaticQuestion: How can Starfleet attack coercion without collapsing the transport system people use to survive?

objectives.summary: Separate coercive contracts and captive movement from ordinary black-market transport.
Protect debtors, witnesses, and crews before enforcement changes the network.
Provide or authorize a lawful alternative to Black Ledger passage.

pressures.summary: The syndicate will destroy records and move captives if it detects a broad operation.
Sable Crossing fears Starfleet occupation more than it trusts its own fragmented courts.
Closing syndicate routes strands refugees and medical transfers immediately.

revelations.summary: Black Ledger is a franchise of cells held together by debt standards and access, not one ship or headquarters.
Nyra Voss believes coercive control is the only way to keep route knowledge from armies.
Halden traded obsolete access codes for witness movement after her crash.

approaches: Target specific cells and protect witnesses
Negotiate debt cancellation for route transfer
Build a public transport alternative first
Seize records through a limited boarding action
Empower Sable courts and local enforcement

outcomes.summary: A lawful transport network undercuts coercive passage while targeted cases proceed.

effects.title: Lawful Passage Capacity
effects.playerSummary: Protected berths, public pricing, witness transfer, and audited route access.

outcomes.summary: Starfleet and local partners dismantle key coercive cells without declaring a general blockade.
Voss accepts a coercive but functional settlement under surveillance.

effects.title: Black Ledger Intelligence
effects.playerSummary: Limited access to syndicate route and debt records at continuing ethical cost.

outcomes.summary: A broad operation triggers record destruction and captive dispersal; rescue and prosecution continue through harder routes.

templates.title: The People Between Maps
templates.summary: A surge of Hadran refugees, deserters, political prisoners, and separated families reaches the March as competing authorities issue contradictory warrants and asylum claims.
templates.playerSummary: A surge of Hadran refugees, deserters, political prisoners, and separated families reaches the March as competing authorities issue contradictory warrants and asylum claims.
templates.directorSummary: Treat the movement as a distributed humanitarian and legal problem. Different groups can be received, moved, registered, protected, exchanged, or refused under different arrangements; the chapter should create persistent obligations rather than one mass choice.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What does a border owe people whose legal identities were defined by the conflict they escaped?

objectives.summary: Separate urgent protection, medical, family, security, and legal needs.
Create distributed shelter and transport capacity.
Establish how Hadran warrants and Starfleet asylum duties will be handled.

pressures.summary: Nysa's Lantern cannot absorb another major convoy without exposing additional sites.
Some arrivals carry weapons, intelligence tasks, or coercive orders.
Family members often hold incompatible statuses and testimony.

revelations.summary: Many warrants were generated from association and residence data rather than individual evidence.
Refugee communities already operate schools, clinics, dispute councils, and route maintenance.
Major Renn has a short high-priority extraction list hidden inside the larger warrant demand.

approaches: Recognize refugee councils as operational partners
Refuse Hadran access and prepare for incursion

outcomes.summary: The March establishes distributed protection with review, family reunification, and local representation.

effects.title: Refugee Councils
effects.playerSummary: Representative partners for shelter, education, family tracing, and route maintenance.

outcomes.summary: Starfleet centralizes screening and protection at Meridian.
A monitored agreement returns some persons and recognizes selected asylum cases.
Capacity or trust fails and a refugee convoy departs through dangerous unrecorded routes.

templates.title: The Officer in the Veil
templates.summary: Halden establishes direct contact from Nysa's Lantern and offers evidence that can expose the Lacuna Protocol, but only if the Aster Vale first protects witnesses and prevents three hostile searches from converging.
templates.playerSummary: Halden establishes direct contact from Nysa's Lantern and offers evidence that can expose the Lacuna Protocol, but only if the Aster Vale first protects witnesses and prevents three hostile searches from converging.
templates.directorSummary: Halden is neither a captive to rescue nor a flawless whistleblower. She continued altering records after her crash, accepted dangerous private bargains, and believes delay was necessary to preserve lives. The player decides her custody, authority, and evidentiary role.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Can an officer be protected as a witness while being held accountable for the methods that made the evidence possible?
templates.sourceSection: Chapter 7: The Officer in the Veil

objectives.summary: Secure the witnesses and route custodians Halden identifies.
Make direct contact without enabling hostile triangulation.
Set Halden's command, legal, and evidentiary status.

pressures.summary: Each communication increases hostile confidence in Halden's location.
Kellan's command status and prior decisions become inseparable from Halden's case.
Halden will act independently if she believes formal custody will expose witnesses.

revelations.summary: Halden has continued changing records to test who exploits each alteration.
The Protocol emerged from overlapping emergency decisions rather than one central conspiracy.
Admiral Holt knew protected chart categories existed but not their scale or criminal penetration.

approaches: Bring Halden aboard under protective custody
Recognize her as a field investigator with limits
Transfer her to independent JAG custody
Keep her at Nysa under Starfleet protection

outcomes.summary: Halden enters protected Starfleet custody with independent evidence controls.

effects.title: Halden Testimony
effects.playerSummary: Direct testimony and a distributed evidentiary key under accountable custody.

outcomes.summary: Halden continues a bounded field audit under the player's operational authority.
Halden remains at Nysa with negotiated Starfleet protection and no formal surrender.
Halden is captured, flees, or breaks contact; her distributed evidence survives through alternate custodians.

templates.title: Far Lantern
templates.summary: The last prewar Starfleet border beacon begins restoring deleted route history, including sanctuary locations, covert wartime movements, private crimes, and evidence of lawful protection.
templates.playerSummary: The last prewar Starfleet border beacon begins restoring deleted route history, including sanctuary locations, covert wartime movements, private crimes, and evidence of lawful protection.
templates.directorSummary: Far Lantern is an archival and infrastructure crisis. The player may repair, isolate, filter, duplicate, destroy, or transfer it, but every choice changes navigation, accountability, and the safety of named communities.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What should survive when one archive contains both the truth and the coordinates of everyone the truth could endanger?

objectives.summary: Stabilize the beacon before automatic restoration or failure.
Classify records by safety, legal value, and consent where possible.
Choose an archive and navigation-custody model.

pressures.summary: Automatic restoration is making more of the archive discoverable over time.
Starfleet, Hadran, Black Ledger, and local custodians converge on the beacon.
The beacon's damaged memory cannot preserve every category through every repair strategy.

revelations.summary: Far Lantern retained signed deltas for nearly every route alteration in the March.
The same technical mechanism was used for sanctuary, intelligence, smuggling, and coercion.
No complete public restoration can avoid exposing people still at risk.

approaches: Preserve evidence but delete live coordinates
Transfer custody to a multi-party commission
Destroy the archive after extracting selected proof

outcomes.summary: Far Lantern becomes a compartmented archive and public safety beacon under plural custody.

effects.title: Far Lantern Archive
effects.playerSummary: A layered historical record with protected coordinates and auditable legal access.

outcomes.summary: The full archive is broadcast and mirrored.
Live route detail is destroyed while a legal record of decisions and abuses survives.
The beacon is destroyed, seized, or rendered unreadable; testimony and distributed fragments become the remaining route to truth.

templates.title: A Border Without Witnesses
templates.summary: Admiral Holt orders the Aster Vale to centralize all chart evidence and withdraw from local custody arrangements just as Hadran forces and Black Ledger cells move against the remaining witnesses.
templates.playerSummary: Admiral Holt orders the Aster Vale to centralize all chart evidence and withdraw from local custody arrangements just as Hadran forces and Black Ledger cells move against the remaining witnesses.
templates.directorSummary: This is an institutional convergence, not a reveal scene. The player must decide whether to obey, challenge, narrow, or reinterpret the order while still protecting civilians and preparing a durable border arrangement.
templates.dramaticQuestion: What does lawful command require when a lawful order would make accountability and protection mutually exclusive?

objectives.summary: Establish the order's scope, urgency, legal basis, and operational consequences.
Protect witnesses and evidence through the period of command dispute.
Create the authority structure that can enter the finale with practical legitimacy.

pressures.summary: Disobedience may end the player's command before the regional crisis resolves.
Hostile actors exploit the reporting dispute to raid sites and remove people.
Senior officers need a clear boundary between lawful challenge and improvised rebellion.

revelations.summary: Holt believes uncontrolled disclosure will collapse border cooperation and destroy careers needed for reform.
One centralized transfer would create a single point of seizure or suppression.
Local councils can hold defined custody responsibilities if Starfleet recognizes them.

approaches: Refuse centralization and preserve distributed custody
Transfer selected duties to local councils
Return Kellan to command while the player leads field protection

outcomes.summary: The player secures a review and preserves evidence through a lawful distributed chain.

effects.title: Protected Evidence Chain
effects.playerSummary: A multi-custodian record robust against seizure, suppression, and single-point failure.

outcomes.summary: Starfleet receives defined categories while protected local custody survives.
The player refuses the order and preserves the network, accepting immediate command jeopardy.
The complete record transfers to Starfleet; the border gains formal clarity but loses local trust and resilience.

templates.title: Draw the Border
templates.summary: Hadran forces, refugee convoys, syndicate cells, local patrols, and Starfleet evidence teams converge across the March while route data and custody systems fail in incompatible ways.
templates.playerSummary: Hadran forces, refugee convoys, syndicate cells, local patrols, and Starfleet evidence teams converge across the March while route data and custody systems fail in incompatible ways.
templates.directorSummary: Assemble the finale from actual state. Use the player's route regime, allies, evidence custody, Halden status, ship condition, and unresolved obligations to configure simultaneous fronts. The finale is not a predetermined battle or council vote.
templates.dramaticQuestion: Can the March become visible enough to protect people without becoming legible enough to control them?

objectives.summary: Prevent mass seizure, disappearance, or exposure of protected populations.
Keep enough routes functioning for rescue, withdrawal, and lawful access.
Prevent or survive the armed convergence without surrendering all border authority to force.
Commit a durable post-crisis navigation, asylum, evidence, and enforcement regime.

pressures.summary: No ship can personally cover every route, site, and convoy.
Every use of the full archive changes the tactical map and the political settlement.
Kellan, Halden, Holt, and the player may each hold different forms of lawful or practical authority.
The ship is capable but not large enough to dominate the theater; alliances and delegated assets determine reach.

revelations.summary: The final Lacuna Protocol is the sum of current custodians, route practices, and accepted authorities, not an artifact waiting to be switched on.
Durable protection requires institutions able to witness and contest decisions without holding every secret.

approaches: Negotiate Hadran withdrawal through verified access
Use limited force to break one convergence point
Abandon selected sites to save people through mobile evacuation

outcomes.summary: Plural custody, protected passage, accountable evidence, and limited enforcement survive the convergence.
The March protects most people through continued partial obscurity and trusted local custodians.
Starfleet restores formal charts, patrol lines, and centralized records at significant local and humanitarian cost.
Traffic remains open but coercion, hidden bargains, and weak accountability define the settlement.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Travel advances time and allows the rest of the March to act.
rules.directorSummary: Advance only fronts with causal readiness. Do not create an event at every travel boundary.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Improved charts increase traffic and attention across previously quiet routes.
rules.directorSummary: High chart confidence is not automatically good or bad; show who gains access and who loses cover.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Shelter, medical, school, and transport capacity show visible strain.
rules.directorSummary: Surface concrete queues, rationing, family separation, or site exposure; do not narrate a raw number.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Hadran patrol concentration and inspection demands increase.
rules.directorSummary: Escalate through signals, patrol posture, warrants, and preparation before armed contact.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Witnesses, debt records, and transport crews begin disappearing from familiar sites.
rules.directorSummary: Move specific assets and people causally; do not erase all evidence or captives at once.

rules.playerFacingSummary: A protected channel offers a plausible route to direct contact with Halden.
rules.directorSummary: The channel must cite existing evidence and should create risk, not deliver the whole truth by narration.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Corrupted historical traffic from Far Lantern becomes detectable across the March.
rules.directorSummary: Release bounded fragments that establish urgency without dumping the archive contents.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Halloway closes its port except to specifically cleared traffic.
rules.directorSummary: The colony remains reachable through diplomacy, local routes, or emergency necessity; closure is not deletion.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Marker drift closes one Gravesend crossing and degrades another.
rules.directorSummary: Leave alternate routes and repair opportunities. A physical route failure should transform plans rather than end the campaign.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Starfleet can no longer defer a decision about Captain Kellan's command status.
rules.directorSummary: Use actual evidence, player conduct, Kellan's condition, and campaign need. Do not predetermine return or relief.

effects.title: Regional Coordination
effects.playerSummary: Local councils and route custodians coordinate without requiring Starfleet to command every action.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Local authorities begin honoring shared decisions even when they lose an individual dispute.
rules.directorSummary: Show legitimacy through voluntary compliance, shared staffing, appeals, and self-initiated support.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Delegation slows, private reservations remain unspoken, and senior staff seek clearer command intent.
rules.directorSummary: Do not make the whole crew mutinous. Select one or two relevant officers and ground concern in observable events.

rules.playerFacingSummary: Ordinary assignments may remain ordinary unless current world state causally changes them.
rules.directorSummary: Do not convert every school, memorial, repair, or family problem into Halden, Hadran, or Protocol material.

cards.title: Campaign Promise
cards.content: The player is the newly promoted Commander and Executive Officer of the U.S.S. Aster Vale. Captain Idris Kellan remains legal commanding officer but begins ashore under inquiry, leaving the player in acting command of a persistent border region whose routes, records, and communities have been deliberately made unevenly visible.

Captain Idris Kellan remains legal commanding officer but begins ashore under inquiry, leaving the player in acting command of a persistent border region whose routes, records, and communities have been deliberately made unevenly visible.

cards.title: Campaign Thesis
cards.content: A border is not only a line on a chart. It is the set of people, institutions, and records allowed to witness who crosses it. Every restoration or erasure distributes access, danger, recognition, and power.
cards.text: A border is not only a line on a chart. It is the set of people, institutions, and records allowed to witness who crosses it. Every restoration or erasure distributes access, danger, recognition, and power.

cards.title: Fixed Truths
cards.content: There is no single mastermind behind the Lacuna Protocol. It emerged from overlapping wartime decisions by Starfleet officers, colony officials, refugee pilots, Hadran defectors, and later criminal exploiters. Halden survived her shuttle crash and continued an audit from protected sites, altering records to observe who exploited each change. Kellan authorized limited protective redactions but did not know the full network or Black Ledger penetration. Holt knew protected chart categories existed but not their scale. Preserve these distinctions.

cards.title: Persistent Open World
cards.content: Locations, routes, factions, witnesses, and fronts continue outside the foreground scene. Travel advances time. The player may respond personally, delegate, delay, refuse, withdraw, or accept that another actor will handle an opportunity. Do not use a chapter cursor to force sequence.

cards.title: Failure Forward
cards.content: Failure creates cost, delay, exposure, injury, damaged trust, route degradation, lost custody, or a transformed objective. It never deletes the only route to campaign-critical truth and never ends a mission through one failed check.

cards.title: Player Agency
cards.content: Never write the player character's dialogue, thoughts, feelings, intentions, or decisions. Present facts, officer advice, procedural competence, time to decide when plausible, and committed consequences.

cards.title: Route Custody Model
cards.content: Model every route through four separable dimensions: physical status/confidence, visibility, access rules, and custodian. Improving one dimension can worsen another. Every route change should identify who gains access, who loses cover, who must maintain it, and how the decision becomes known.

cards.title: Erasure Distinctions
cards.content: Keep absence, privacy, non-recognition, protective redaction, coerced disappearance, archival loss, and criminal falsification distinct. Do not treat every missing record as the same moral or legal fact.

cards.title: Calm Content
cards.content: School routes, maintenance, memorials, family contact, agriculture, ordinary customs, and scientific calibration may remain calm. Do not attach every side assignment to Halden, Hadran forces, Black Ledger, or the Protocol.

cards.title: Faction Autonomy
cards.content: Factions act from their goals, information, resources, risk tolerance, and timing. They do not wait offstage for the player and do not exist merely to counter the latest player choice.

cards.title: Knowledge Boundaries
cards.content: No actor begins with the whole campaign truth. Holt, Kellan, Halden, Voss, Sorn, Renn, local councils, and the crew each possess bounded and sometimes mistaken knowledge. Do not let one confrontation produce omniscient exposition.

cards.title: Command Briefings
cards.content: Senior-staff briefings should distinguish known facts, inference, unknowns, risks, procedural options, and decisions reserved to command. Officers may disagree, but avoid full-crew debates in every scene.

cards.title: Refusal Is Consequential
cards.content: Refusing or abandoning an assignment is valid. Record who acts instead, what pressure advances, what obligations remain, and what the crew and region can reasonably observe.

cards.title: Command Bearing
cards.content: Command Bearing moments arise when the player accepts responsibility, preserves an officer's dignity, acts with moral clarity under pressure, shares risk, admits uncertainty honestly, or holds a principled line at cost. Do not award for rank display alone.

cards.title: Canon Era
cards.content: The opening year is 2371. Voyager is missing in the Delta Quadrant and its fate is unknown. The Dominion War has not begun. Keep technology, politics, uniforms, and Starfleet assumptions consistent with early 2370s continuity without making canon figures the center of this original region.

cards.title: Aster Vale Mission Profile
cards.content: The New Orleans-class Aster Vale is a compact frontier frigate with strong sensors, modular mission pods, credible defensive capability, limited evacuation volume, and no capacity to dominate the entire March. Its best solutions combine survey work, small-team operations, diplomacy, and local partnerships.

cards.title: Aster Vale Constraints
cards.content: The ship carries limited humanitarian stores, only two standard shuttles plus mission-pod deployment craft, modest brig and interview space, and wartime mission-pod bypasses that create EPS and data-integrity risk. Sustained regional control requires allies and delegated work.

cards.content: Kellan remains legal captain while the player initially exercises acting command under written delegation. The player has genuine authority over the ship, patrol, away teams, rules of engagement, and mission priorities. Kellan's later return must not silently revoke accumulated agency.

cards.title: U.S.S. Aster Vale
cards.content: A New Orleans-class frontier frigate serving as patrol ship, charting platform, diplomatic venue, and temporary refuge.
cards.text: A New Orleans-class frontier frigate serving as patrol ship, charting platform, diplomatic venue, and temporary refuge.

cards.title: U.S.S. Aster Vale: Director
cards.content: The ship is the campaign's mobile command base. Its location, passenger load, brig use, chart custody, shuttle availability, and mission-pod status must remain persistent. It can control one crisis at a time and cannot replace local institutions. Everyday texture: Watch rotations overlap with route reviews, temporary passengers occupy training spaces, and the wardroom becomes a courtroom, planning cell, or family meeting room depending on the day.
